projects.title: ResumeAI Tailor — ATS-Optimized Resume Generator
projects.summary: A free web application that helps job seekers dramatically increase their interview callback rate by generating ATS-optimized, tailored resumes in seconds using AI.

overview.scope: A web application that accepts a user's resume and a job description, then uses AI to generate a tailored, ATS-optimized .docx resume for download.
overview.responsibility: Full-stack development, including frontend UI/UX, backend API development, AI integration, and prompt engineering.

projects.problem: Most resumes are rejected by Applicant Tracking Systems (ATS) before a human reads them due to a lack of keyword optimization and proper formatting. Job seekers miss opportunities not because they are unqualified, but because their resumes fail automated screening.
constraints: Must use only free-tier APIs (Groq, Gemini) with no paid services.
No database or user authentication in the MVP.
Must be deployable on Vercel's free tier.
Serverless function execution timeout of 10 seconds.
projects.approach: The project was built following a detailed Product Requirements Document and a step-by-step build guide. The architecture separates concerns into a reactive frontend, three distinct backend API routes for parsing, AI generation, and file export, and a robust AI integration layer with a primary/fallback mechanism to ensure high availability and performance.

architecture.data: AI integration via Groq API (Llama 3) and Google Gemini API

execution: Developed a multi-step backend process: 1. Parse uploaded resume (PDF/DOCX). 2. Call AI with resume text and job description. 3. Generate a downloadable .docx file.
Engineered a robust AI prompt to ensure consistent, high-quality JSON output for resume content.
Built a modular component-based UI with React and Next.js for a clean user experience.
Implemented a primary (Groq) and fallback (Gemini) AI service pattern to handle potential API failures.
Ensured the generated .docx files were strictly ATS-compliant by avoiding columns, tables, and other problematic formatting.
outcomes: Dramatically reduces the time to tailor a resume from hours to seconds.
Generates highly-optimized resumes designed to achieve a 70%+ ATS keyword match score.
Produces professionally formatted, ATS-safe .docx files ready for immediate submission.
Provides users with clear feedback on what was changed and why via a match score and keyword list.
projects.reflection: This project was a deep dive into full-stack development with a modern Jamstack architecture. It highlighted the power of LLMs in solving practical user problems and the importance of robust prompt engineering to get reliable, structured output from AI. Implementing the fallback API pattern was a key learning in building resilient systems.

projects.title: Parachute Consulting Solutions — Kinetic Minimalism Marketing Website
projects.summary: Leading the design and development of an 8-page marketing and lead generation website for an executive advisory firm, featuring a distinctive Kinetic Minimalism design direction with scroll-driven animations and full Sanity CMS control.

overview.scope: An 8-page marketing and lead generation website for an executive advisory firm, with the deliberate goal of converting the right visitors into direct conversations with the founder.
overview.responsibility: Leading design and development end-to-end, from information architecture to CMS implementation and motion design.

projects.problem: The client needed a distinctive online presence that differentiates them from generic consulting firms, with complete control over all content post-launch without touching code.
constraints: Must feature distinctive Kinetic Minimalism design direction
Full CMS control over all copy, navigation labels, form placeholders, and section content
Cover 4 service sectors: aviation, operations, culture, and safety
Scroll-driven animations throughout
projects.approach: Designed the website with a focus on leading with the client's problem, positioning PCS as the answer, and introducing the founder as proof. Built a fully CMS-driven content architecture using Sanity IO with embedded studio for complete client control.

architecture.frontend: Next.js 14 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, ShadCN UI

execution: Engineered homepage information hierarchy through multiple iterations to lead with the client's problem, position PCS as the answer, and introduce the founder as proof
Built fully CMS-driven content architecture using Sanity IO with embedded studio
Implemented Kinetic Minimalism design direction with scroll-driven animations using Framer Motion
Created responsive layouts covering 4 service sectors: aviation, operations, culture, and safety
outcomes: Gave client complete control over all copy, navigation labels, form placeholders, and section content post-launch
Created distinctive visual identity that stands out from generic consulting firms
Improved how the brand reads to its target audience through deliberate information hierarchy
projects.reflection: This project emphasized the importance of information architecture and content hierarchy in conversion-focused websites. Working with Sanity IO's embedded studio gave me deeper experience in headless CMS architecture and content modeling for marketing sites.

projects.title: SmartRetry — Headless Fintech Marketing Platform
projects.summary: Architected and delivered a production-grade, multilingual fintech marketing platform built on Next.js 15 and deployed on Cloudflare Workers, integrating a headless WordPress CMS, automation workflows, and performance-focused infrastructure.

overview.scope: A modular, CMS-driven fintech marketing platform with multilingual support, dynamic content modules (directory, FAQ, glossary, blog), SEO optimization, automation workflows, and production-grade deployment.
overview.responsibility: Owned frontend architecture, CMS modeling, multilingual routing, automation integration, performance optimization, deployment workflow, and production reliability.

projects.problem: The startup required a scalable marketing platform capable of rapid iteration, structured SEO content expansion, and multilingual support. The system needed to balance animation-heavy UX, dynamic CMS content, automation workflows, and production reliability — all within startup constraints.
constraints: Startup timelines with evolving requirements
Headless WordPress behind a private Cloudflare Tunnel (no public CMS access)
Need for multilingual and RTL support
Animation-heavy UI without sacrificing performance
Deployment on Cloudflare Workers (not traditional Node hosting)
Strict security, monitoring, and SEO requirements
projects.approach: I designed the platform around a modular App Router architecture using Next.js 15 with server-side rendering for SEO and performance. WordPress was consumed headlessly via REST through a private Cloudflare Tunnel. Automation workflows were integrated using Make.com webhooks for CRM synchronization. I prioritized strict TypeScript boundaries, CMS structure clarity, and performance discipline to ensure scalability under evolving requirements.

architecture.frontend: Next.js 15 App Router + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS
architecture.cms: Headless WordPress accessed securely via Cloudflare Tunnel
architecture.automation: Make.com webhooks integrated with Monday CRM for contact and newsletter workflows
architecture.monitoring: Sentry for error tracking and performance monitoring

execution: Built multilingual routing system with dynamic locale-based pages and RTL support
Implemented CMS-driven modules including directory, FAQ, glossary, blog, and structured content blocks
Integrated Lottie animations with controlled client rendering to preserve performance
Implemented React Hook Form + Zod validation for robust form handling
Connected contact and newsletter forms to Make.com via webhooks for CRM automation
Configured secure WordPress access through Cloudflare Tunnel (no public CMS endpoints)
Deployed on Cloudflare Workers with optimized SSR and caching strategies
Implemented structured SEO metadata, Open Graph tags, canonical URLs, and JSON-LD schemas
Integrated Sentry for production monitoring and release tracking
outcomes: Delivered a scalable marketing platform capable of rapid content iteration
Enabled secure headless CMS integration without exposing backend infrastructure
Automated CRM workflows via Make.com, reducing manual data handling
Maintained performance and SEO integrity despite animation-heavy UI
Established a production-ready architecture suitable for future feature expansion
projects.reflection: This project reinforced that content modeling decisions directly shape frontend complexity in headless systems. It also highlighted the importance of balancing visual interactivity with performance discipline. Deploying on Cloudflare Workers required careful SSR considerations, strengthening my understanding of edge deployment constraints.

projects.title: Z Aviation Consulting — Multi-site Web Delivery & Headless Strategy
projects.summary: Led end-to-end delivery of multiple company websites, combining stakeholder discovery, PRDs, design system work, modern frontend architecture, headless CMS integration, and VPS production releases.

heroImage.alt: Z Aviation Consulting websites project preview

overview.scope: A multi-site web delivery program that covered requirements, UX planning, design system creation, scalable frontend builds, headless CMS integration, deployment, and ongoing production support.
overview.responsibility: Owned the delivery lifecycle end-to-end: stakeholder discovery → PRD/specs → UX flows & design system → build → release → support, while coordinating tasks and quality reviews as acting team lead.

projects.problem: The organization needed reliable, business-critical websites delivered on real timelines, with a structure that supports ongoing iteration, stakeholder clarity, and maintainable long-term ownership — not one-off builds.
constraints: Multiple sites delivered in phases aligned to business timelines
Non-technical stakeholders required clear requirements and predictable delivery
Production reliability and release coordination on VPS infrastructure
Balancing speed with long-term maintainability across multiple codebases
projects.approach: I ran delivery like a system: clarify requirements early, document decisions, create reusable design and UI patterns, then implement with a scalable architecture that supports future changes. I prioritized reducing ambiguity for stakeholders while keeping the technical foundation clean and maintainable for long-term iteration.

architecture.frontend: Next.js + Tailwind CSS with reusable UI patterns
architecture.data: Supabase integration for selected backend workflows where needed
architecture.deployment: VPS hosting with production release discipline and ongoing maintenance

execution: Led stakeholder discovery and documented requirements, PRDs, and technical specifications
Designed user flows, wireframes, and a design system in Figma to align UI/UX with business goals
Built and delivered multiple websites using WordPress and a headless CMS strategy for phased rollout
Coordinated implementation work, task tracking, and code reviews to maintain quality and consistency
Deployed and maintained production websites on VPS infrastructure, including server configuration and release handling
Provided ongoing technical support and troubleshooting as the primary point of contact for stakeholders
outcomes: Delivered multiple production websites aligned to business timelines through phased execution
Improved stakeholder clarity through documented PRDs, specs, and consistent delivery structure
Established a scalable foundation using modern frontend architecture and headless CMS workflows
Strengthened production reliability through disciplined VPS deployments and release management
projects.reflection: This work reinforced that multi-site delivery succeeds when the workflow is designed as deliberately as the code. Clear requirements, reusable UI patterns, and release discipline reduce chaos and create compounding speed over time.

projects.title: SOCA — AI-Powered Task Management System
projects.summary: A full-stack task management web application that leverages generative AI to automatically break complex tasks into actionable subtasks, reducing cognitive overload and improving execution clarity.

heroImage.alt: SOCA AI-powered task management system preview

overview.scope: A web-based task management system that allows users to create, manage, and complete daily tasks, enhanced with AI-powered subtask generation.
overview.responsibility: End-to-end architecture, database design, authentication flow, AI integration, and frontend implementation.

projects.problem: Users often struggle to execute large or vague tasks because they lack structure and clarity. Traditional task managers rely entirely on manual task breakdown, which can increase friction and procrastination.
constraints: Limited infrastructure (PHP + MySQL stack)
API usage limits and response latency from AI providers
Need to maintain responsive UI while handling async AI calls
Authentication and data isolation per user
projects.approach: I designed SOCA as a structured task manager first, then layered AI capabilities on top. The system stores core tasks in a relational database and, upon user request, calls the generative API to intelligently chunk large tasks into smaller actionable subtasks. I prioritized keeping AI generation optional and non-blocking to maintain usability even if external API calls fail.
execution: Designed relational database schema for users, tasks, and subtasks
Implemented secure user authentication with Gmail login integration
Built full CRUD functionality for tasks (create, edit, delete, mark as complete)
Integrated Cohere API and LLaMA-based models for automated subtask generation
Handled asynchronous AI responses and dynamically updated UI without full page reload
Ensured user-level task isolation and secure data handling
outcomes: Reduced cognitive load by automatically converting large goals into manageable subtasks
Improved task clarity and execution structure for end users
Demonstrated integration of generative AI within a traditional PHP full-stack environment
Built a functional AI-assisted productivity system from scratch
projects.reflection: If extending SOCA further, I would refactor the architecture into a more modern framework (e.g., Next.js + API layer), implement rate-limiting and caching for AI responses, and introduce smarter prioritization algorithms based on task metadata.

projects.title: Library Course-Based Computer Usage Monitoring System
projects.summary: Designed and built a desktop monitoring system that tracked computer usage by enrolled course, improving resource visibility and administrative efficiency within a university library environment.

overview.scope: A desktop-based monitoring system that tracked active computer users by academic course and generated structured usage reports for administrative analysis.
overview.responsibility: System design, UI implementation, data modeling, reporting logic, and on-site support.

projects.problem: The library lacked structured visibility into computer usage patterns across different academic courses. Monitoring was manual, time-consuming, and prone to record inaccuracies, limiting the administration’s ability to allocate resources effectively.
constraints: On-site infrastructure limitations (desktop-only environment)
Non-technical staff as primary system users
Need for reliable Excel-compatible reporting
Minimal tolerance for system downtime during operating hours
projects.approach: I approached the project by first mapping the real-world workflow of library staff and identifying bottlenecks in data recording and reporting. The system was designed to simplify user registration tracking while automatically structuring data for export. Usability and clarity were prioritized over technical complexity to ensure seamless adoption by non-technical users.

architecture.deployment: Local desktop deployment within library environment

execution: Designed user interface tailored for quick staff input and monitoring
Implemented structured tracking of users categorized by academic course
Built automated Excel export functionality using Pandas for reporting
Optimized data handling to reduce manual encoding and errors
Provided on-site software and hardware troubleshooting support
outcomes: Improved visibility into departmental computer usage trends
Reduced manual reporting workload for library staff
Increased record accuracy through structured data capture
Delivered a practical internal system aligned with operational workflows
projects.reflection: This project reinforced the importance of aligning technical solutions with real-world operational processes. Designing for non-technical users required clarity and restraint — simplicity proved more valuable than adding advanced features.
